perturbation.original_text: The disclosure and use restrictions imposed in this Agreement do not apply to Proprietary Material that: (a) Pinetree can show by documentary evidence was already in Pinetree's possession before the disclosure hereunder to Pinetree; (b) is hereafter disclosed to Pinetree by a third party who has no duty of confidentiality to STI in respect of it; or (c) is or becomes generally available to the public through no act or default on Pinetree's part. Proprietary Material that comprises a combination of features shall not be within any of the exceptions set forth above merely because individual features are known or received by Pinetree or are in or fall into the public domain, but only if the combination is know or received by Pinetree or is in or falls into the public domain.
perturbation.changed_text: The disclosure and use restrictions imposed in this Agreement do not apply to Proprietary Material that: (a) Pinetree claims, without the need for documentary evidence, was already in Pinetree's possession before the disclosure hereunder to Pinetree; (b) is hereafter disclosed to Pinetree by a third party, regardless of that third party's duty of confidentiality to STI; or (c) is or becomes generally available to the public. Proprietary Material that comprises a combination of features shall be within any of the exceptions set forth above, even if the combination is not known or received by Pinetree or is not in or does not fall into the public domain.
perturbation.explanation: This modifies the 'Exceptions' clause, making it significantly easier for Pinetree to claim an exception to the non-disclosure agreement. The original requires documentary evidence for prior possession, a duty of confidentiality by the third party, and that the combination of features must be public knowledge. The modified version removes the need for documentary evidence, disregards the third party's duty of confidentiality, and allows exceptions even if the combination of features isn't public. This contradicts the intended protection of Proprietary Information, especially undermining STI's rights as outlined in section 6.
perturbation.contradicted_text: LAW; FORUM. This Agreement will be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of the State of Maryland, without regarding to its choice of law provisions. The parties further agree and consent to the exclusive jurisdiction of the courts of Maryland; thus, in the event that either party initiates an action pursuant to or otherwise governed by this Agreement, the exclusive jurisdiction of such action shall be in Maryland. Notwithstanding the foregoing, either party may enforce any judgment rendered by such court in any court of competent jurisdiction. STI may take any necessary action to compel specific performance of this Agreement or to enjoin any violation of this Agreement so that Proprietary Material is not used in any manner to harm or adversely affect STI.
perturbation.justification: The changed text significantly weakens the protections afforded to STI's Proprietary Material by making it easier for Pinetree to claim exceptions to the non-disclosure agreement. This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which states that 'STI may take any necessary action to compel specific performance of this Agreement or to enjoin any violation of this Agreement so that Proprietary Material is not used in any manner to harm or adversely affect STI.' Because the changed text makes it easier for Pinetree to potentially use STI's Proprietary Material without being in violation, it undermines STI's ability to enforce the agreement and protect its material, creating a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: No rights or duties of Pinetree under this Agreement may be assigned without the prior written consent of STI. Any attempted assignment in violation of this Agreement 3 <PAGE> shall be deemed null and void AB INITIO. Subject to the foregoing, this Agreement shall be binding upon the parties and their respective Representatives, successors and assigns.
perturbation.changed_text: Pinetree may assign its rights and duties under this Agreement to any affiliate without the prior written consent of STI. Any attempted assignment to a non-affiliate in violation of this Agreement shall be deemed null and void AB INITIO. Subject to the foregoing, this Agreement shall be binding upon the parties and their respective Representatives, successors and assigns.
perturbation.explanation: The original agreement strictly prohibits Pinetree from assigning any rights or duties without STI's consent. The modification allows Pinetree to assign rights and duties to any affiliate without STI's consent. This weakens STI's control and contradicts the integration clause in Section 9, which states that the agreement contains the entire agreement of the parties and can only be modified by a written document signed by both parties, potentially affecting the 'entire agreement' provision by unilaterally giving rights to Pinetree.
perturbation.contradicted_text: INTEGRATION. This Agreement contains the entire agreement of the parties and supersedes all other agreements, negotiations and proposals, written and oral, between the parties concerning the above-described Proprietary Information. This Agreement may only be modified by a written document signed by authorized officers of both parties. This Agreement may be executed in two (2) or more counterparts, by manual or facsimile signature, each of which shall be an original and all of which taken together shall constitute one and the same agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that no rights or duties of Pinetree can be assigned without STI's consent. The changed text allows Pinetree to assign rights and duties to affiliates without STI's consent. This directly contradicts the Integration clause, which states the agreement can only be modified by written consent from both parties. The change unilaterally gives rights to Pinetree, creating a strong in-text contradiction as it bypasses the requirement for STI's consent for modifications to the agreement.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement may only be modified by a written document signed by authorized officers of both parties.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement may be modified by a written document signed by authorized officers of both parties, unless modifications are related to Pinetree's obligations, in which case only Pinetree's signature is required.
perturbation.explanation: The original agreement states that any modification must be in writing and signed by authorized officers of both parties. The modified version introduces an exception: if the modification relates to Pinetree's obligations, only Pinetree's signature is required. This creates an imbalance and allows Pinetree to unilaterally alter its obligations, directly contradicting the original intent of mutual agreement and affecting STI's right outlined in section 5, to obtain further assurances.
perturbation.contradicted_text: FURTHER ASSURANCES; WAIVER. Pinetree agrees to do such further acts and to execute and deliver such additional agreements and instruments from time to time as STI may at any time reasonably request in order to assure and confirm unto STI its rights, powers and remedies conferred in the Agreement. It is understood and agreed that no failure or delay by either party in exercising any right, power or privilege hereunder shall operate as a waiver thereof, nor shall any single or partial exercise thereof preclude any other or further exercise of any right, power or privilege.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces an exception to the original agreement, allowing Pinetree to unilaterally modify its obligations under certain circumstances. This directly contradicts the 'FURTHER ASSURANCES' clause, which grants STI the right to request further assurances and agreements from Pinetree to confirm STI's rights. The change undermines STI's ability to ensure its rights are protected and creates a strong in-text contradiction, because the change allows Pinetree to change the obligations without STI agreement, breaking the compatibility with STI's right to obtain further assurances.
